scope: The SwornAgent board command now discovers every locally available valid release board through one read-only catalog oracle, ignores malformed noncanonical historical topology records, preserves canonical release-worktree fail-closed semantics, and reports deterministic source and durability provenance in aggregate and named output.

reachability.evidence: Live project-root evidence after commit 2cca001: `GOFLAGS=-buildvcs=false /usr/local/go/bin/go run ./cmd/sworn board --json` exited 0 and emitted 17 releases, including `2026-07-17-ref-aware-board-discovery` from `refs/heads/release-wt/2026-07-17-ref-aware-board-discovery`; the named form exited 0 with one track and no `releases` envelope. `TestBoardCLIAllRefsCatalogSkipsInvalidNoncanonicalTopology` reproduces the former failure with a lexically earlier malformed noncanonical bare-string board, proves invalid-only historical records are omitted, proves a valid lower-priority noncanonical record wins, and proves an unrelated named query stays reachable. The required mutation transcript remains: forcing discovery to use only HEAD made TestBoardCLIAllRefsCatalogStateEvidenceReachability fail with `releases=0, want 2` (exit 1); restoration made it pass (exit 0).

delivered.item: AC-01 â€” no-filter board discovery returns a bytewise release-sorted all-ref catalog, including non-HEAD releases, selected source refs, and farthest elected slice evidence; malformed noncanonical historical records no longer prevent a real repository catalog from being emitted.
delivered.evidence: cmd/sworn/board_test.go:TestBoardCLIAllRefsCatalogStateEvidenceReachability and TestBoardCLIAllRefsCatalogSkipsInvalidNoncanonicalTopology; live project-root command in reachability evidence

delivered.item: AC-02 â€” topology source selection follows canonical local, canonical remote, local noncanonical, then remote noncanonical priority with bytewise tie-breaking among valid direct records.
AC-03 â€” malformed, missing, or identity-mismatched canonical records still fail the catalog closed without partial output or fallback selection.
AC-04 â€” one shared state-evidence election selects lifecycle and attention high water marks and labels committed versus dirty-working-tree provenance.

AC-05 â€” named release queries use the same catalog evidence while retaining the established release/tracks JSON envelope and one-board text form, even when unrelated noncanonical records are malformed.
AC-06 â€” ref discovery remains read-only and the full targeted and repository-wide Go gates are clean.
